entries.title: Managed runs now support multi-service actions with clearer log views
entries.summary: Managed runs can now launch composite actions that run multiple services at once. The logs experience has been redesigned with per-run tabs, service-specific sub-tabs, and a merged view so it is easier to follow activity across complex runs.

entries.title: Managed run cleanup and stop behavior is more reliable
entries.summary: Run lifecycle handling is more robust during launches, removals, and deleted actions, reducing cases where partial failures or buffered output could leave behind stale runs or inconsistent log state.

entries.title: Sub-tickets now appear in a collapsible tree
entries.summary: Ticket details now show the full sub-ticket hierarchy in a recursive tree instead of a flat list, making it easier to navigate nested work. Expanded and collapsed state is remembered per ticket, and hover previews remain available alongside existing selection, context menu, and drag-to-chat actions.

entries.title: Project-specific browser panel visibility
entries.summary: Browser panel visibility is now remembered separately for each project, so switching projects preserves each workspace’s own browser setup instead of applying one global state.

entries.title: Fixed ticket delete and archive dialogs reopening on the wrong ticket
entries.summary: Resolved an issue where deleting or archiving a sub-ticket could leave the confirmation dialog open after navigating back, creating a risk of accidentally acting on the parent ticket.

entries.title: Claude MCP refresh is more reliable
entries.summary: Refreshing Claude MCP integrations now uses an improved reload flow, helping status updates appear more consistently when plugins change.

entries.title: Ticket descriptions can now be updated incrementally
entries.summary: Improved ticket editing so descriptions and acceptance criteria can be patched without replacing the entire content, making ticket updates smoother and more reliable.

entries.title: Dynamic chat UI artifacts
entries.summary: Added support for dynamic chat UI artifacts, letting conversations surface richer generated UI content directly in the chat experience.

entries.title: Claude MCP server status is now reflected in chat
entries.summary: The chat experience now surfaces Claude MCP server availability so you can better understand which integrations are connected and ready to use while working in a session.

entries.title: Codex now defaults to GPT-5.5
entries.summary: Added GPT-5.5 as a built-in Codex model and made it the default selection for new Codex sessions, while keeping GPT-5.4 and older variants available to choose manually.

entries.title: Improved Codex Session Resume Reliability
entries.summary: Codex sessions now resume more reliably by avoiding a readiness race that could interrupt or mis-handle restored sessions.

entries.title: Fixed Embedded Browser Project Scoping
entries.summary: The embedded browser now stays correctly scoped to the active project, preventing browser activity from leaking into or using the wrong project context.

entries.title: Embedded browser DevTools now dock to the side
entries.summary: Opening DevTools from the embedded browser now places the inspector beside the T3 Code window by default, reducing window clutter while still allowing users to switch dock positions or undock it manually.

entries.title: Embedded Browser Reload, Zoom, and DevTools Shortcuts
entries.summary: Embedded browser tabs now support reload shortcuts, keyboard zoom controls, native trackpad pinch zoom, and DevTools shortcuts for inspecting the active tab.

entries.title: More Reliable Worktree Development State
entries.summary: Development worktrees now keep separate local data directories, reducing cross-branch state conflicts and making branch-specific development sessions more predictable.

entries.title: More Transparent Claude Run Status
entries.summary: Claude runs now surface terminal reasons and hook lifecycle output more clearly in the conversation timeline, while hiding successful hook exit codes to reduce noise.

entries.title: Claude Usage Tier Labeling
entries.summary: Claude rate limit and usage displays now label the design usage tier more clearly.

entries.title: Clearer Claude Context Usage
entries.summary: Claude conversations now show a more useful context usage breakdown, with a simplified popover that makes it easier to understand how the context window is being used.

entries.title: Claude Opus With Extra-High Reasoning
entries.summary: Claude support now includes Opus with the extra-high reasoning option, expanding the model choices available when starting or configuring agent work.

entries.title: Faster Scheduled Task Settings
entries.summary: Scheduled task settings now load more quickly, making it easier to browse and manage task details without waiting on heavier project data.

entries.title: Image attachments for tickets
entries.summary: Tickets now support image attachments with drag-and-drop and file-picker uploads, thumbnail previews, click-to-zoom viewing, and live updates when artifacts change.

entries.title: Inline attachment renaming
entries.summary: Image attachment titles can now be renamed directly from the ticket detail view, including a warning when changing a file extension.

entries.title: Promote chat images to tickets
entries.summary: Images from chat attachments can now be added to tickets, making it easier to turn conversation context into tracked ticket artifacts.

entries.title: Improved Claude Session Continuity
entries.summary: Claude sessions now preserve the active provider context more reliably, helping resumed conversations and stop actions stay tied to the correct session.

entries.title: Ticket archiving
entries.summary: Tickets can now be archived instead of deleted, including bulk or nested ticket workflows. Archived tickets are hidden from active board views and can be restored or permanently deleted from the new Archived tickets area in Settings.

entries.title: More reliable session resume
entries.summary: Session restarts now preserve conversation context more reliably when provider profiles differ between the saved session and the restart request.

entries.title: Moved editor launch options into sidebar menus
entries.summary: Editor open actions are now available from sidebar menus, keeping conversation headers cleaner while preserving quick access to preferred editor workflows.

entries.title: Browser Access Moved to the Board Navigation
entries.summary: The embedded browser now has its own globe button in the board navigation bar, making it available independently from the cards and list view controls. Existing browser view preferences are preserved automatically.

entries.title: Simplified Model Selection for Runs
entries.summary: Removed duplicate per-ticket implementer and reviewer model overrides so model choices are controlled from the run configuration in one predictable place.

entries.title: Navigate Back to the Board Faster
entries.summary: Added a Back to board shortcut in the ticket header for quicker navigation from ticket details to the board.

entries.title: Filter Board by Status
entries.summary: Added a Status filter to the board toolbar, letting users show or hide entire status columns in both card and list views. Status selections are saved per project alongside existing filters.

entries.title: Reduced Browser Navigation Noise
entries.summary: Canceled navigations in the embedded browser are now hidden, keeping browser feedback cleaner during interrupted or redirected page loads.

entries.title: Browser guidance adapts to the current runtime
entries.summary: T3 Code now tailors browser behavior guidance to the active runtime, improving reliability across the embedded desktop browser and web-based environments.

entries.title: Embedded browser tabs are now visible and persistent
entries.summary: The embedded browser now includes a tab strip with favicons, close controls, a new-tab button, keyboard shortcuts, and cleaner blank-tab address handling. Tabs persist across restarts, and links that open new windows now open as embedded tabs instead.

entries.title: Agents can work across multiple browser tabs
entries.summary: The embedded browser now supports agent-controlled tab creation, switching, listing, and closing, with a five-tab limit per project to keep behavior predictable.

entries.title: PDF export works in the embedded browser
entries.summary: The embedded browser can now export pages to PDF reliably instead of failing when the previous print path was unavailable.

entries.title: Chat focus is preserved during browser navigation
entries.summary: Agent-driven browser navigation and reloads no longer steal focus from the chat input, so you can keep typing while the embedded browser loads pages in the background.

entries.title: Embedded browser dialogs no longer interrupt the app
entries.summary: Pages that trigger alert, confirm, or prompt dialogs are now handled inside the embedded browser without blurring or blocking the rest of T3 Code. Browser-click actions were also corrected so a single click only fires once.

entries.title: Managed runs shut down more cleanly
entries.summary: Stopping a managed run now cleans up its process tree more reliably, reducing the chance of leftover background processes after a run ends.

entries.title: More reliable embedded browser automation
entries.summary: Embedded browser sessions now keep element references consistent across multi-step agent actions, so flows like inspecting a page and then clicking a referenced element are less likely to fail.

entries.title: Improved browser interactions in desktop sessions
entries.summary: Browser tools now handle clicks, form filling, scrolling, hover, style inspection, and keyboard shortcuts more predictably inside the embedded desktop browser.

entries.title: Fixed embedded browser popups and overlays
entries.summary: Dialogs, menus, popovers, and other overlays now suspend the embedded browser correctly, preventing browser content from interfering with foreground UI.

entries.title: Fixed Browser Tool Crashes
entries.summary: Resolved a production issue where browser actions such as snapshots, screenshots, page evaluation, and navigation could crash instead of completing successfully.

entries.title: Native Embedded Browser for Board Mode
entries.summary: Added a native embedded browser experience that runs inside the desktop app, enabling browser automation to work directly with an in-app browser instead of relying only on a headless browser host.

entries.title: Smarter Browser Host Selection
entries.summary: T3 Code can now choose between the embedded desktop browser and the existing headless browser host, giving projects more flexibility while keeping browser actions routed through a consistent automation flow.

entries.title: More Reliable Embedded Browser Recovery
entries.summary: Improved recovery when the embedded browser’s debugging connection detaches, reducing the chance that browser automation gets stuck after DevTools conflicts or connection interruptions.

entries.title: More Robust Provider Session Cleanup
entries.summary: Refined provider session cleanup so stopped or restarted sessions are handled more predictably, improving reliability around long-running agent activity and recovery paths.

entries.title: Improved run resume reliability
entries.summary: Resuming orchestration runs is now more reliable when a provider turn is already in progress. Long-running turns are tracked by activity instead of a fixed wall-clock timeout, reducing stuck runs and preventing runner state from drifting away from the provider session.

entries.title: Added provider profiles
entries.summary: T3 Code now supports provider profiles, making it easier to choose and use different configured provider setups from the chat and settings experience.

entries.title: Added project-scoped browser automation for agents
entries.summary: Agents can now use an in-process Chromium browser to navigate pages, inspect content, interact with elements, take screenshots, and run batched browser actions. Browser sessions are isolated per project, preserve cookies and login state across restarts, and use stable element references for more reliable automation.

entries.title: Improved browser tooling reliability in desktop builds
entries.summary: Desktop builds now include the required Chromium runtime for browser automation, reducing setup failures and making browser tools work more predictably in packaged apps.

entries.title: Clearer REST API error details
entries.summary: REST API failures now return more complete underlying error information, making issues easier to understand when browser tools, managed runs, prompts, scheduled tasks, or ticketing actions fail.

entries.title: Improved sub-ticket selection for orchestration
entries.summary: The orchestration selector now groups sub-tickets under their parent ticket, shows parent progress with a count indicator, and keeps rows in place while toggling selections. This makes nested ticket execution easier to review and less jumpy to use.

entries.title: Sessions can now restart themselves when tools need a refresh
entries.summary: Agents can restart their own provider session when newly installed tools need to load or a tool call becomes stuck. The restart happens after the current turn finishes, preserves conversation context, and keeps the continuation out of the visible timeline.

entries.title: More control when starting orchestration runs
entries.summary: Added per-run options to include or exclude selected tickets, override implementer and reviewer models, skip review, and set maximum review rounds before starting a run.

entries.title: Bulk ticket deletion from the board
entries.summary: Added a bulk delete action to the board selection bar with confirmation, making it faster to remove multiple selected tickets at once from card or list views.

entries.title: Gemini is now a first-class provider
entries.summary: Added Gemini support across chat and orchestration, including provider selection, settings, attachments, authentication detection, MCP server discovery, quota usage reporting, runtime access controls, and parity with existing Codex and Claude workflows.

entries.title: Cleaner ticket links in chat
entries.summary: Improved chat rendering so ticket links still appear as ticket badges even when models wrap parts of the link syntax in backticks.

entries.title: App-styled context menus
entries.summary: Replaced native operating system context menus with consistent in-app menus that match T3 Code's design language across environments.

entries.title: Smoother Sidebar Startup
entries.summary: The sidebar no longer animates during the initial render, reducing visual distraction when the app first loads.

entries.title: Improved Ticket Board Navigation and Filtering
entries.summary: Ticket management is easier to scan and refine with board/list view switching, richer filters, clearer ticket details, and preserved board context when starting new threads.

entries.title: Expanded Ticket Board Views and Filtering
entries.summary: Ticket management now supports priority, label, and search filters, plus a choice between board and list layouts. Ticket details and descriptions are also easier to read.

entries.title: Expanded ticket board filtering and views
entries.summary: Tickets can now be filtered by priority, labels, and search, with a new option to switch between board and list layouts. Priority icons also make ticket urgency easier to scan at a glance.

entries.title: Claude Opus 4.7 support
entries.summary: Added Claude Opus 4.7 as an available model option, including support for Opus aliases that resolve to the new model.

entries.title: Faster Orchestration Loading
entries.summary: Orchestration views now load child threads in parallel, reducing wait time when opening runs with multiple related threads.

entries.title: Faster orchestration loading
entries.summary: Opening orchestration runs is now more responsive, with child threads loaded on demand and only the most recent activity hydrated immediately.

entries.title: Clearer Resource Management Settings
entries.summary: Conversation cache and idle session timeout controls are now grouped in a dedicated Resource Management section with clearer descriptions, making memory and inactive session behavior easier to understand.

entries.title: Configurable Thread Content Cache
entries.summary: A new Settings > General option lets users choose how much thread content T3 Code keeps cached, from unlimited to 1, 2, 4, or 8 GB. The default cache limit is now 1 GB to improve responsiveness while keeping memory use bounded.

entries.title: Scheduled Task Views Use Lighter Data Loading
entries.summary: Scheduled task proposal and settings views now use the lighter snapshot data path, improving responsiveness when browsing tasks in larger projects.

entries.title: On-Demand Thread Loading
entries.summary: Opening a thread now hydrates its messages and related panels on demand, with clearer loading behavior across chat, diffs, plans, and timelines.

entries.title: Faster Startup for Large Workspaces
entries.summary: T3 Code now starts with a lightweight orchestration snapshot and loads detailed thread content only when needed, reducing initial load work while preserving session state.

entries.title: Faster scoped data loading
entries.summary: Managed runs, scheduled tasks, and prompt-related views now use more targeted lookups, reducing unnecessary data loading and improving responsiveness as project history grows.

entries.title: Faster prompt management for scoped projects
entries.summary: Prompt management now looks up scoped projects directly, improving responsiveness and reducing unnecessary background work when managing project-specific prompt settings.

entries.title: Preserved Conversation Context After Idle Stops
entries.summary: Idle or manually stopped sessions now preserve their resume data, so returning to a session after inactivity no longer starts a fresh conversation or loses prior context.

entries.title: Kanban ticket details now remember your scroll position
entries.summary: Returning to a previously viewed ticket in the Kanban detail view restores your earlier scroll position, making it easier to continue where you left off.

entries.title: Improved provider lifecycle diagnostics
entries.summary: Provider sessions now produce more detailed lifecycle logging, making it easier to investigate context loss, recovery behavior, and other session-level issues when troubleshooting.

entries.title: Orchestration now skips tickets that are already done or canceled
entries.summary: Starting an orchestration run now ignores tickets already in a terminal state, preventing unnecessary execution threads from being created for work that does not need to run.

entries.title: Improved ticket link rendering in chat
entries.summary: Fixed chat messages so ticket links wrapped in backticks now render correctly as interactive ticket links.

entries.title: Cleaner ticket details and acceptance criteria editing
entries.summary: Refined ticket management screens with a simpler ticket detail layout and tighter acceptance criteria controls for a cleaner editing experience.

entries.title: Improved ticket description readability
entries.summary: Ticket descriptions are easier to scan with larger body text and more consistent heading spacing.

entries.title: Refined ticket detail headers for clearer context
entries.summary: Ticket detail views now present key context more clearly, including parent-ticket relationships, inline ticket identifiers, a compact origin thread badge, and better placement for labels and worktree details.

entries.title: More reliable session recovery after restarts
entries.summary: Fixed a restart issue that could leave behind orphaned CLI processes and corrupt the active session state. This improves stability when sessions are restarted, helping prevent unexpected stops and lost conversation context.

entries.title: Per-run prompt overrides for orchestration
entries.summary: Orchestration runs now support prompt overrides scoped to a single run, so you can adjust instructions for one execution without changing your project-wide prompt setup. Active runs also appear in the Prompts settings view, and edits made during a run can take effect without affecting other runs.

entries.title: Improved stability when resolving ticket identifiers
entries.summary: Fixed a crash that could occur when ticket identifier requests were missing or invalid, resulting in clearer failures instead of unexpected errors.

entries.title: Diff panel is clearer when working across multiple repositories
entries.summary: The diff view now keeps repository tabs separate from turn navigation, groups changes by repository, and adds repo badges in All repos mode so similarly named files are easier to distinguish.

entries.title: Archived threads are excluded from startup recovery
entries.summary: Archived threads no longer appear in the startup snapshot, reducing clutter and keeping recovery state focused on active work.

entries.title: Claude profile rate limits now update correctly
entries.summary: Fixed the rate limit meter so it reflects the currently selected Claude profile when you switch providers or change the dropdown selection.

entries.title: Markdown formatting in review summaries and comments
entries.summary: Review output now renders markdown in summaries and comment bodies, improving readability for formatted feedback.

entries.title: Added editable admin prompts in Settings
entries.summary: Settings now include an Admin Prompts section for managed runs, scheduled tasks, and ticketing, making these instructions configurable without code changes.

entries.title: Fixed POST-based admin actions that could fail unexpectedly
entries.summary: Resolved a request parsing issue that could break POST requests for ticketing, managed runs, and scheduled tasks.

entries.title: Improved stability during long-running sessions
entries.summary: Reduced memory growth in board and orchestration-heavy workflows by bounding cached orchestration data and improving cleanup of background provider processes.

entries.title: Added automatic cleanup for idle sessions
entries.summary: Sessions can now be stopped automatically after a configurable idle timeout, with new Settings options for disabling cleanup or choosing common timeout lengths. Idle sessions remain resumable when you return.

entries.title: Ticket editing now includes rich text formatting and inline acceptance criteria
entries.summary: Ticket descriptions now use a rich text editor with formatting controls, and acceptance criteria can be added, edited, and removed directly in the ticket view.

entries.title: Plan accept mode now switches back to chat correctly after auto-accept
entries.summary: Fixed an issue where threads could stay stuck in plan accept mode after the server automatically accepted a plan.

entries.title: Internal tools now use a more reliable REST connection
entries.summary: T3 Code now uses a plain REST API for built-in tools, reducing agent connection failures and making tool access more consistent across sessions.

entries.title: Faster board loading when switching threads
entries.summary: Improved board responsiveness when moving between threads and projects by reducing redundant loading work and eliminating stalls that could delay the view by several seconds.

entries.title: Nested ticket dependency trees are now supported
entries.summary: Tickets can now model multi-level dependency trees, with dependent work ordered correctly to preserve execution flow.

entries.title: Nested ticket dependencies now run in order
entries.summary: Orchestration now supports nested ticket dependency trees, building run plans from the selected ticket hierarchy so dependent work executes in the intended sequence.

entries.title: Improved orchestration for nested ticket dependencies
entries.summary: Fixed ordering issues in orchestration when tickets include nested dependencies, making execution more reliable for complex dependency chains.

entries.title: Clearer ticket context throughout orchestration
entries.summary: Improved how ticket context is carried through orchestration so related ticket details are surfaced more consistently across planning, timelines, and ticket views.

entries.title: Changelog History Is Preserved Across Clean Builds
entries.summary: The in-app changelog now retains existing release history more reliably after clean builds, so past updates remain visible instead of being dropped.

entries.title: New default idea label and template
entries.summary: New workspaces now include a built-in idea label and Idea template to make capturing early-stage concepts faster.

entries.title: Move sub-tickets directly to a board
entries.summary: Added a move-to-board action for sub-tickets, available from ticket details and context menus, with selection state cleared after the move.

entries.title: Improved orchestration thread handling
entries.summary: Refined orchestration behavior across chat and thread navigation, including clearer handling for parent, review, waiting-child, and cascade-delete states. Connection fallback behavior was also streamlined to keep recovery states more predictable.

entries.title: Orchestration launch handoff on startup is more dependable
entries.summary: Improved startup handoff behavior when launching orchestration, reducing friction during session transitions.

entries.title: Ticket comments can be deleted more cleanly
entries.summary: Improved ticket comment deletion behavior for a smoother comment management experience.

entries.title: Orchestration setup and model display were clarified
entries.summary: Refined orchestration-related settings and confirmation flows to present model choices and launch behavior more clearly.

entries.title: Prompt management in Settings was polished
entries.summary: Improved the prompts settings experience, including editing flows and panel behavior.

entries.title: Task and ticket details were improved in Settings
entries.summary: Refined task-related settings and ticket detail views for a clearer management workflow.

entries.title: Managed runs controls received usability refinements
entries.summary: Updated managed runs controls and settings to make run management clearer and more predictable.

entries.title: Scheduled tasks management was refined across settings and proposal flows
entries.summary: Improved the scheduled tasks experience in Settings and related proposal flows, with clearer handling across task details and controls.

entries.title: Connection status handling is more reliable in chat
entries.summary: Refined connection-state behavior so chat surfaces recover and present connection issues more consistently.

entries.title: Archived threads can be deleted in bulk from Settings
entries.summary: Improved archived thread management with bulk deletion support in Settings, making large cleanups faster.

entries.title: Added a built-in changelog in Settings
entries.summary: T3 Code now includes a dedicated changelog view in Settings so you can review recent product updates directly in the app.

entries.title: Polished chat and navigation behavior
entries.summary: Markdown file links, multi-repo diffs, sidebar status, and related chat interactions now behave more consistently across everyday workflows.

entries.title: Introduced global ticket labels and reusable templates
entries.summary: You can now manage shared ticket labels and templates from Settings, making it faster to standardize and create work across projects.

entries.title: Improved orchestration timeline clarity and live status feedback
entries.summary: Timeline events now use cleaner labels, link ticket identifiers directly, preserve more context around pauses and reviews, and show active work more clearly while runs are in progress.

entries.title: Made startup recovery status more accurate
entries.summary: The temporary "Was working" recovery state now clears as soon as live activity resumes, reducing stale status indicators after reconnects or restarts.

entries.title: Startup recovery now restores stale work more safely
entries.summary: The app now detects stale threads and orchestration runs on startup and recovers them more reliably after interruptions or restarts.

entries.title: Ticket thread links are simplified
entries.summary: Ticket details now focus on the origin thread link, reducing clutter and making the primary conversation path clearer.

entries.title: Orchestration timelines are easier to scan
entries.summary: Orchestration activity is now grouped into thread-style blocks for implementation and review, improving readability while preserving message ordering and context.

entries.title: Ticket mentions in chat are now clickable
entries.summary: Ticket references rendered in chat can now be opened directly, making it faster to jump from conversations and orchestration updates into the relevant ticket.

entries.title: Composer and skills selection flows are more polished
entries.summary: Refinements to composer waiting states and skills selection make chat setup feel smoother and more consistent.

entries.title: Ticket summaries refresh after label changes
entries.summary: Ticket summary content now updates after label edits so ticket views stay in sync with the latest metadata.

entries.title: Provider selection better preserves preferred setups
entries.summary: Improved provider picker behavior to better support saved or profiled provider selections when composing chats.

entries.title: Board interactions are more resilient
entries.summary: Improved board and ticket flows around drag-and-drop, multi-select moves, sidebar resizing, and board-context handling so ticket management behaves more predictably during complex interactions.

entries.title: Review results now wait for finalized output
entries.summary: Orchestration review handling now waits for the completed review response before parsing it, reducing stale or incomplete review results.

entries.title: Ticket management interactions were expanded and refined
entries.summary: Board and ticket-management flows received usability improvements across editing, decomposition, worktree-related actions, markdown rendering, previews, and management mode switching.

entries.title: Ticket details are cleaner and more focused
entries.summary: Ticket detail views now use a simpler layout, show the originating thread more directly, and refresh relationship details more predictably.

entries.title: Sub-tickets can now be promoted to the main board
entries.summary: You can now move sub-tickets into the top-level board, with support for both individual and bulk promotion flows.

entries.title: Follow-up orchestration runs have better resume and re-review handling
entries.summary: Orchestration now supports improved resume behavior for fresh agents and better follow-up reviews, including later review passes that account for changes since earlier feedback.

entries.title: Review suggestions now appear more consistently as comments
entries.summary: Review output is now normalized so suggestion-style feedback is shown as comments more reliably, including compatibility with older review responses.

entries.title: Collapsed sidebar is easier to reopen across the app
entries.summary: A dedicated toggle now appears when the sidebar is collapsed, making it easier to reopen navigation from chat, settings, and board views.

entries.title: Orchestration progress is clearer inside child threads
entries.summary: Child threads now show the active orchestration progress in the header, including the current item or timeline label, and that label can be used to jump between orchestration and child-thread views more easily.

entries.title: Paused orchestration runs can resume with a fresh agent
entries.summary: You can now resume a paused orchestration run by restarting it with a fresh child agent directly from the orchestration header.

entries.title: Orchestration ticket ordering is now more stable
entries.summary: Ticket ordering is now more consistent during orchestration planning, reducing unexpected reshuffling when items are created close together.

entries.title: Orchestration setup and review flows are clearer and more resilient
entries.summary: The orchestration confirmation flow now shows cleaner provider names, better explains when automated review is disabled, supports skipping review threads when review cycles are off, and keeps ticket loading working more reliably if snapshot data is unavailable.

entries.title: Review parsing is more tolerant of formatted output
entries.summary: Review results now parse more reliably when returned as prose-wrapped or fenced JSON, reducing failures from differently formatted responses.

entries.title: Ticket identifiers are now project-scoped
entries.summary: Ticket numbering and lookup now respect project boundaries, improving consistency when working across multiple projects.

entries.title: New threads can inherit the current board context
entries.summary: Starting a new thread from the same project now preserves relevant board context like ticket stack and scroll position, making it easier to continue work without reorienting.

entries.title: Prompt customization for orchestration is now available
entries.summary: You can now manage orchestration prompts from Settings, with global defaults, project-specific overrides, validation, preview support, and prompt resolution applied at run time.

entries.title: Tickets can now stay linked to their conversation threads
entries.summary: T3 Code now tracks ticket-to-thread relationships so ticket workflows stay better connected to the conversations and branches around them.

entries.title: Board and ticket state now stay scoped to the current project
entries.summary: Project boards, ticket lookups, and orchestration actions now stay tied to the selected project, reducing stale state and cross-project mixups.

entries.title: Orchestration model selection is now explicit and configurable
entries.summary: You can now choose implementer and reviewer models in Settings and override them per ticket. Orchestration uses those selections consistently instead of switching models at runtime.

entries.title: Sidebar now shows active orchestration work
entries.summary: The sidebar now reflects live orchestration activity, marks active runs as working, and uses the updated “My Projects” label for clearer navigation.

entries.title: Comment deletion is now available in ticket settings
entries.summary: Ticket comments and replies can now be removed with confirmation, including clearer context before deleting threads with replies.

entries.title: Added a dedicated orchestration timeline experience
entries.summary: Orchestration threads now open in a specialized timeline view with progress controls, child-thread navigation, waiting states for upcoming work, and a redesigned interface that better matches the rest of the app.

entries.title: Added automated review for orchestration runs
entries.summary: Orchestration can now produce automated review results, with new settings and in-app review output that surfaces pass, fail, and blocked outcomes directly in the timeline.

entries.title: Improved orchestration reliability and resume behavior
entries.summary: Orchestration runs now persist and order more reliably, startup issues were resolved, resume flows were hardened, and parent tickets expand into subtasks more accurately when preparing runs.

entries.title: Introduced orchestration runs for ticket execution
entries.summary: You can now start orchestration runs from the board, review execution order before launch, and have tickets processed sequentially with clearer handling for blocked or interrupted work.

entries.title: Safer sidebar actions for archived threads
entries.summary: Project actions now handle archived threads more predictably, reducing the chance of unexpected behavior when managing older conversations.

entries.title: Richer ticket references and dependency details
entries.summary: Chat and ticket detail views now show ticket names alongside identifiers, making linked tickets and dependencies easier to recognize at a glance.

entries.title: Expanded ticket editing and planning controls
entries.summary: Tickets now support inline title and description editing, optional worktree tracking, a blocked status, acceptance criteria, and improved multi-select behavior in board views.

entries.title: Ticket actions and agent handoff workflows expanded
entries.summary: Added a Decompose action that opens a new chat thread with the ticket attached, making it easier to break larger tickets into scoped sub-tickets.

entries.title: Kanban interactions are more stable and informative
entries.summary: Refined drag reordering behavior, improved rollback handling, added dependency titles and statuses in ticket detail, and refreshed ticket detail views when related tickets change.

entries.title: Chat side panels can now be resized
entries.summary: Diff and file explorer panels in chat now support draggable resizing with saved widths for a more adjustable workspace.

entries.title: Ticketing works more consistently with human-readable ticket IDs
entries.summary: Ticket actions now accept and return readable ticket identifiers more consistently, improving compatibility across ticketing workflows.

entries.title: Improved readability across changed files and ticket content
entries.summary: Raised contrast in the changed-files tree and upgraded ticket rendering with markdown support, badges, and more polished detail layouts.

entries.title: Kanban supports bulk ticket selection and clearer epic progress
entries.summary: Added multi-ticket selection for faster board actions, surfaced epic progress, and improved navigation through nested ticket relationships.

entries.title: Fixed startup issues affecting some existing installations
entries.summary: Resolved a migration problem that could prevent the app from starting on certain databases, including production desktop installs.

entries.title: Ticket labels are now scoped per project
entries.summary: Ticket labels are now isolated by project, reducing cross-project collisions and keeping board organization clearer in multi-project use.

entries.title: Improved runtime visibility for stalled work and recoverable failures
entries.summary: The app now surfaces more structured runtime status information, making it easier to understand stalled threads, dispatch flow, and recoverable failures while work is in progress.

entries.title: Kanban board now supports drag-and-drop and ticket attachments in chat
entries.summary: You can now reorder tickets, move them across columns, and drag tickets into the chat composer as structured attachments for quicker agent workflows.

entries.title: New Board view for ticket management
entries.summary: Introduced a Board layout with a Kanban ticket board and integrated chat panel, plus a Chat/Board toggle in the sidebar. Ticket management has moved out of Settings into this new workflow.

entries.title: File links now open directly in the editor at the right line
entries.summary: Clicking linked files in chat now reveals the file in the explorer and opens the editor at the requested line for faster navigation.

entries.title: Project-level system prompts now carry across orchestration
entries.summary: Added project-specific system prompt support so sessions can retain and apply project guidance more consistently, including when project context changes.

entries.title: Enabled diff word wrap by default
entries.summary: Diff views now wrap long lines by default for easier reading without horizontal scrolling.

entries.title: Added an agent tools delivery mode setting
entries.summary: Added a new setting to choose between native tool delivery and HTTP endpoint delivery for agent tool access, giving more control over how integrated tools are exposed.

entries.title: Added Tickets management in Settings
entries.summary: Introduced a Tickets workspace for tracking work with list and detail views, status and priority controls, acceptance criteria, comments, history, and live updates.

entries.title: Improved ticket navigation and compatibility with older data
entries.summary: Project selection now persists while moving between ticket views, and older tickets with missing or malformed acceptance criteria load correctly.

entries.title: Improved clipboard behavior when copying code snippets
entries.summary: Copying from editor views now preserves snippet metadata more reliably, with normalized pasted text handling for better matching.

entries.title: Improved runtime responsiveness and navigation reliability
entries.summary: Reduced UI overhead in thread-heavy sessions, sped up bulk archive actions, prioritized stop and interrupt commands, and fixed navigation timing when opening forked threads.

entries.title: Moved plan auto-accept handling to the server
entries.summary: Proposed plans in auto-accept mode now complete more reliably after successful turns, with acceptance handled centrally on the server.

entries.title: Added managed run inference results
entries.summary: Managed runs can now generate and store structured inference output through a shared execution path, improving consistency across supported providers.

entries.title: Renamed Cron Jobs to Scheduled Tasks
entries.summary: Updated the product language from Cron Jobs to Scheduled Tasks across the app for clearer, more approachable automation controls.

entries.title: Improved session stability for Claude resumes and restarts
entries.summary: Fixed issues that could interrupt Claude session recovery, trigger unnecessary restarts after reopening the app, or cause instability under load.

entries.title: Added scheduled task automation with in-app management
entries.summary: Introduced recurring task scheduling with a dedicated Settings experience for creating, editing, and reviewing automated runs, plus chat proposal cards for accepting new schedules from conversations.

entries.title: Better recovery after disconnects and stalled requests
entries.summary: The app now handles WebSocket disconnects more gracefully and provides clearer feedback when requests are taking unusually long, reducing confusion during temporary connection issues.

entries.title: Improved startup and recovery behavior
entries.summary: Startup and recovery flows are more resilient, including better handling for replay retries and launching the app from a chosen working directory.

entries.title: Managed Runs introduced for long-running project services
entries.summary: T3 Code can now track long-running project scripts and services as Managed Runs, with health-aware status, logs, inline review for proposed actions, and controls available to supported agents.

entries.title: Threads can now be moved between projects
entries.summary: You can now move threads to a different project from the sidebar, making it easier to reorganize work without losing conversation history.

entries.title: Rate-limit visibility is clearer, including Claude profile warnings
entries.summary: Provider usage tracking now surfaces rate-limit status and OAuth tier details more clearly, including per-profile Claude warnings, backoff notices, and improved meter messaging in chat.

entries.title: Forked threads recover more safely
entries.summary: Forked thread recreation is more resilient when the original provider session is no longer available, helping preserve fork behavior across restarts and recovery flows.

entries.title: Plan-accept mode added for supported models
entries.summary: Supported models can now use a plan-accept workflow that turns approved plans into implementation automatically, with updated composer controls to expose plan support more clearly.

entries.title: Skill attachments added to the composer
entries.summary: You can now attach discovered skills directly in the composer, with attachment state preserved when drafts are restored.

entries.title: Composer now shows available MCP servers automatically
entries.summary: T3 Code now detects MCP servers from your provider configuration and lets you pick from available servers directly in the composer. The old runtime access toggle has been removed in favor of this streamlined flow.

entries.title: More reliable server-managed terminal startup
entries.summary: Project worktree setup and terminal launch context are now handled more consistently, improving session startup and restore behavior.

entries.title: Resizable plan sidebar
entries.summary: The plan sidebar can now be resized and remembers its width, making it easier to tailor the workspace to your preferred layout.

entries.title: New built-in file explorer and editor
entries.summary: Threads now include a full file explorer and editor with tabs, split panes, quick open, markdown preview, save shortcuts, and persistent layout state for working directly in your project.

entries.title: Multiple Claude profiles can now be used side by side
entries.summary: T3 Code now automatically discovers separate Claude profile setups and shows each one as its own provider, with independent sign-in state, models, and labels throughout the app.
